info.title: Supabase API (v2)
info.description:
Supabase API generated from the OpenAPI specification.
Visit [https://supabase.com/docs](https://supabase.com/docs) for a complete documentation.

402.description: This feature requires the Pro, Team, or Enterprise organization plan.

500.description: Failed to create a log drain

post.summary: Create a log drain for a project

x-badges.name: Only available on Pro, Team, Enterprise

500.description: Failed to delete a log drain

post.summary: Previews transferring a project to a different organizations, shows eligibility and impact
Transfers a project to a different organization

aws_account_id.description: The AWS account ID this PrivateLink share is associated with.

account_name.description: Human-readable name for the AWS account.

status.description:
- `CREATING`: The PrivateLink resources and the Association are in the process of being created. The PrivateLink Share cannot be accepted yet.
  - `READY`: The PrivateLink resources have been created and the PrivateLink Share can be accepted for the duration of 12h after sharing. See `shared_at`.
  - `ASSOCIATION_REQUEST_EXPIRED`: The PrivateLink Share has not been accepted within the 12h time limit. This association can now be deleted.
  - `ASSOCIATION_ACCEPTED`: The PrivateLink Share was successfully accepted.
  - `CREATION_FAILED`: The PrivateLink resources failed to create. This likely means something went wrong on Supabase's and and support should be contacted.
  - `DELETING`: The PrivateLink resources and the Association are in the process of being deleted. The PrivateLink Share cannot be accepted yet.

shared_at.description: The time and date at which the AWS Resource Share Association was requested from Supabase. `null` means that the association was not yet requested while the PrivateLink Association is pending.

500.description: Failed to retrieve AWS accounts for project

get.summary: List AWS accounts attached to the project PrivateLink share

post.description: Adds an AWS account to the project's PrivateLink configuration and schedules the AWS resources to be created.

aws_account_id.description: The AWS account ID to add to the project PrivateLink share.

account_name.description: Optional human-readable name for the AWS account.

aws_account_id.description: The AWS account ID this PrivateLink share is associated with.

account_name.description: Human-readable name for the AWS account.

status.description:
- `CREATING`: The PrivateLink resources and the Association are in the process of being created. The PrivateLink Share cannot be accepted yet.
  - `READY`: The PrivateLink resources have been created and the PrivateLink Share can be accepted for the duration of 12h after sharing. See `shared_at`.
  - `ASSOCIATION_REQUEST_EXPIRED`: The PrivateLink Share has not been accepted within the 12h time limit. This association can now be deleted.
  - `ASSOCIATION_ACCEPTED`: The PrivateLink Share was successfully accepted.
  - `CREATION_FAILED`: The PrivateLink resources failed to create. This likely means something went wrong on Supabase's and and support should be contacted.
  - `DELETING`: The PrivateLink resources and the Association are in the process of being deleted. The PrivateLink Share cannot be accepted yet.

shared_at.description: The time and date at which the AWS Resource Share Association was requested from Supabase. `null` means that the association was not yet requested while the PrivateLink Association is pending.

402.description: This feature requires the Team, or Enterprise organization plan.

500.description: Failed to add AWS account to PrivateLink share

post.summary: Add an AWS account to the project PrivateLink share

delete.description: Removes an AWS account from the project's PrivateLink configuration. Cleans up the associated AWS resources.

parameters.name: aws_account_id
parameters.description: AWS account ID used in PrivateLink association

500.description: Failed to remove AWS account from PrivateLink share

delete.summary: Remove an AWS account from the project PrivateLink share

get.description: Returns a cursor-paginated list of organization members including their roles and project-scoped permissions.

mfa_enabled.description: Whether Multi-Factor Authentication is enabled for this member

is_sso_user.description: Whether this member is a Single Sign-On user

name.description: Role name. For project-scoped roles this is the base role name.

scope.description: Whether this role applies org-wide or is scoped to specific projects for the user.

projects.description: Project refs this role is scoped to. Empty array for org-level roles.

roles.description: Roles assigned to this member. Includes both org-level and project-scoped roles.

first.description: URL path to the first page if available.

prev.description: URL path to the previous page.

next.description: URL path to the next page.

last.description: URL path to the last page if available.

patch.description: Assigns an org-wide role when projects is omitted, or creates a project-scoped assignment when projects is provided. Uses an org-level role template id from GET /v2/organizations/{slug}/roles. Stale role assignments are automatically cleaned up: if a role no longer has any projects, it is deleted; overlapping project assignments in other roles are automatically removed to avoid duplication.

role.description: Role name to assign. Must be one of: owner, administrator, developer, read-only. Must be on a Team or Enterprise plan to use the read-only role.

projects.description: The projects to assign a project-scoped role for. If omitted, assigns an org-wide role.

name.description: Role name. For project-scoped assignments this is the base role name.

scope.description: Whether this role applies org-wide or is scoped to specific projects for the user.

projects.description: Project refs this role is scoped to. Empty array for org-level roles.

402.description: This feature requires the Enterprise organization plan.

500.description: Failed to assign organization member role

patch.summary: Assign or change an organization member role

get.description: Returns a list of org-level roles for the organization.

post.description: Creates member invitations for an organization. Each invitation can have different role and project scope settings.

role.description: Role name to assign. Must be on a Team or Enterprise plan to use the read-only role.

projects.description: The projects to limit a user to. If omitted, user will have org-wide access with the provided role.

402.description: This feature requires the Enterprise organization plan.
